focus: Micro-Phonetics Refinement — Dark L Mastery Across All Contexts
focus_ar: تحسين الصوتيات الدقيقة — إتقان الـ L الغامقة في كل السياقات
arabic_challenge: You learned dark L in L2 Week 5. This week refines it to Silver/Gold-tier precision (Table 11) — ensuring the tongue-back-raising motion for syllable-final L survives in unfamiliar vocabulary, humor/sarcasm delivery, complex consonant clusters, emotional speech, and fully spontaneous conversation, matching this week's Humor & Sarcasm vocabulary theme.

target_sounds: dark L in unfamiliar, never-drilled words

isolation.instructions: Test dark L on words you've likely never specifically drilled before.
isolation.instructions_ar: اختبر الـ L الغامقة على كلمات محتمل ماتدربتش عليها قبل كده.
practice: colossal, plausible, dismissal, principal, animal, universal (dark L at syllable end)

sentence_practice: It's a colossal, plausible reason for the dismissal.
The principal made a universal rule about every animal.
A plausible, colossal, universal animal instinct.
daily_drills.record_this: It's a colossal, plausible reason for the dismissal — the principal made a universal rule about every animal.
daily_drills.self_check: Did dark L apply automatically to these unfamiliar words at syllable-end?

target_sounds: dark L in sarcastic/humorous delivery (thematic tie-in)

isolation.instructions: Apply dark L while delivering sarcastic remarks, since exaggerated comedic delivery sometimes disrupts subtle articulatory habits.
isolation.instructions_ar: طبّق الـ L الغامقة وانت بتقدم تعليقات ساخرة، لأن الأداء الكوميدي المبالغ فيه ممكن يعطل عادات نطقية دقيقة أحيانًا.
practice: Oh, TOTALLY brilliant! (sarcastic, dark L on 'total')

sentence_practice: Oh, TOTALLY brilliant! What a FEEL-good story.
Brilliant, absolutely brilliant, a truly beautiful mess.
daily_drills.record_this: Oh, TOTALLY brilliant! What a FEEL-good, beautiful, wonderful story — just wonderful.
daily_drills.self_check: Did the dark L quality survive the exaggerated, sarcastic emphasis?

target_sounds: dark L in complex consonant clusters

isolation.instructions: Test dark L before another final consonant, in more advanced vocabulary than L2's practice set.
isolation.instructions_ar: اختبر الـ L الغامقة قبل صوت ساكن نهائي آخر، في مفردات أكثر تقدمًا من مجموعة تمارين المستوى الثاني.
practice: pulse, results, adults, insults (dark L + cluster)

sentence_practice: The results showed adults' pulse rates rising after insults.
Adults felt insults affected their pulse and results.
The pulse quickened after the results and the insults.
daily_drills.record_this: The results showed adults' pulse rates rising after insults — the pulse quickened noticeably.
daily_drills.self_check: Did dark L hold up in these more complex final-consonant clusters?

target_sounds: dark L under genuine emotional/reactive speech

isolation.instructions: Test dark L while reacting with genuine surprise or amusement to something (real or imagined), since authentic reactions can disrupt careful articulation.
isolation.instructions_ar: اختبر الـ L الغامقة وانت بترد بمفاجأة أو تسلية حقيقية على شيء، لأن ردود الفعل الحقيقية ممكن تعطل النطق الحذر.
practice: React genuinely (out loud) to something you find funny or surprising, watching for dark L in your reaction words.

pair: genuine reaction with dark L intact
genuine reaction with dark L lost

daily_drills.record_this: (Free speech: react genuinely and out loud to something funny or surprising you recall or imagine.)
daily_drills.self_check: In your authentic reaction, did dark L survive on any relevant words?

target_sounds: dark L in fully spontaneous 60-second speech

isolation.instructions: The ultimate test: 60 seconds of spontaneous speech, checking dark L automaticity throughout.
isolation.instructions_ar: الاختبار النهائي: ٦٠ ثانية كلام عفوي، لفحص تلقائية الـ L الغامقة بالكامل.
practice: Speak spontaneously about anything for 60 seconds, ideally including some personal/emotional content.

pair: fully automatic dark L in spontaneous speech
dark L lost without conscious effort

daily_drills.self_check: What percentage of dark-L opportunities did you execute correctly without conscious effort?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review: unfamiliar words, sarcasm delivery, consonant clusters, genuine emotion, full spontaneity
challenge_sentences: It's a colossal, plausible reason — oh, TOTALLY brilliant, what a feel-good, beautiful, wonderful, universal animal instinct!
The principal's pulse quickened after the results and the insults — a truly wonderful, beautiful mess.
Brilliant, absolutely brilliant — the adults' results were colossal and universal.
daily_drills.record_this: It's a colossal, plausible reason — oh, TOTALLY brilliant, what a feel-good, beautiful, wonderful story! The principal's pulse quickened after the results.
daily_drills.self_check: Score your dark-L consistency across this demanding combined-challenge passage.

test_yourself.instructions: Record 60-90 seconds of spontaneous speech including at least one genuine humorous or sarcastic moment.
test_yourself.passage: (Free speech: 60-90 seconds on any topic, including a genuine attempt at humor or sarcasm.)

sounds_to_check.dark L automaticity: check throughout for consistency without conscious effort

scoring_guide.90_100: (Gold/Platinum tier) Dark L completely automatic and invisible, even under sarcasm and genuine emotion.
scoring_guide.70_89: (Silver tier) Dark L holds up in nearly all contexts; rare lapses under pressure.
scoring_guide.50_69: Dark L reliable in calm speech but drops under emotional/comedic pressure.
scoring_guide.below_50: Dark L still requires conscious effort — return to L2 Week 5 for foundational review.

for_arabic_speakers: Following the same Table 11 mastery-tier refinement structure as Week 1's flap T, this week deep-refines dark L instead.

Comedy and sarcasm are genuinely useful stress-tests for pronunciation mastery, because they require you to focus your attention entirely on timing and delivery, not on the sounds themselves — mastery reveals itself when the sounds survive despite not being the focus.
This same Days 1-5 refinement structure continues for schwa (Week 3) and vowel reduction/general fluency (Week 4).
supplementary_notes.recording_requirement: Record Day 5 and Day 7 — compare to Week 1's flap-T mastery recordings to track overall Table 11 tier progress.
